data.title: Sweden's Nordnet Q1 profit hits record on trading activity, new customers
data.description:
Overview

  • Sweden-based digital investment platform's Q1 operating profit and income reached record highs

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 rose to SEK 3.32 from SEK 3.13 yr/yr

  • Net savings and new customer additions among highest since 2020/2021, driven by strong trading

Outlook

  • Nordnet expects full-year costs to be in line with target of about 8% annual increase

  • Company says focus remains on implementing AI for internal processes and customer offerings

Result Drivers

  • NET SAVINGS AND CUSTOMER GROWTH - Co said record net savings and 77,500 new customers were key drivers of quarterly results, per CEO Rasmus Järborg

  • TRADING ACTIVITY AND FUND SAVINGS - Strong growth in brokerage and funds attributed to high trading activity and continued high fund savings

  • NET INTEREST INCOME - Turnaround in net interest income due to rising market interest rates and increased deposits

data.title: Norway's Tomra Systems Q1 revenue rises, but drop in margins drags EPS
data.description:
Overview

  • Norway resource optimization firm's Q1 revenue rose 9% yr/yr to 334 mln EUR

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 fell to 0.02 EUR from 0.03 EUR

  • Recycling segment revenue declined 19% amid continued weak order intake and challenging market

Outlook

  • Company says Recycling continues to face challenging market conditions with low order intake

  • Tomra expects cost reduction program in Recycling to gradually improve profitability

  • Company sees positive market sentiment continuing in Food segment

Result Drivers

  • COLLECTION GROWTH - Revenue growth in Collection driven by steady performance in existing markets and new installations in Poland and Portugal

  • RECYCLING DECLINE - Recycling revenue fell due to recent low order intake and a challenging market environment

  • PRODUCT MIX IMPACT - Gross margin negatively affected by short-term product mix in all segments and low volumes in Recycling

data.title: MPS chief considering Generali stake sale, FT reports
data.description:
Monte dei Paschi di Siena chief Luigi Lovaglio is considering selling a 7.4 billion euro ($8.64 billion) stake in Generali to help fund the acquisition of Banco BPM , the Financial Times reported on Friday.

Lovaglio, who was reinstated as CEO earlier this month after settling a boardroom clash that followed last year's takeover of rival Mediobanca, ultimately wants to sell a 13% stake in Generali owned by Mediobanca, the FT reported, citing people familiar with the matter. MPS had acquired the stake last year.

The proposed sale would be to unnamed long-term Italian investors, with the proceeds helping fund a tie-up between MPS and Banco BPM, the newspaper reported.

Reuters could not immediately verify the report. MPS and Generali did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

Lovaglio had led the 16 billion euro ($18.68 billion) Mediobanca acquisition, but was pushed out last month after presenting in February a strategy that envisaged a swift merger of Mediobanca into MPS to boost savings.

Investors are split on the merits of selling Mediobanca’s Generali stake, although there is broader support for a deal with Banco BPM, the FT reported.

“The priority for the government is for the Generali stake to end up in safe hands because it considers the insurer a national strategic asset,” one of the people told FT.

Another option under discussion is to distribute the stake in Generali to MPS shareholders by way of a special dividend rather than selling it, the FT said.

($1 = 0.8565 euros)

data.title: Signify's first-quarter sales fall due to weak US dollar
data.description:
The world's biggest lighting maker Signify reported a 12% drop in its quarterly nominal sales to 1.27 billion euros ($1.48 billion) on Friday, dragged by currency effects related to the depreciation of the U.S. dollar.

The Dutch company confirmed its full-year guidance for a core profit margin of between 7.5% and 8%, but declined to provide a sales forecast.

($1 = 0.8563 euros)

data.title: French and Benelux stocks-Factors to watch
data.description:
Below are company-related news and stories from France and Benelux which could have an impact on the region's markets or individual stocks.

ACCOR :

French hotel group Accor reported Q1 revenue of 1.31 billion euros with RevPAR up 5.1%, noting the impact of the Iran war on Middle East business activity was more pronounced from mid-March onwards.

ADYEN :

Dutch payments company Adyen announced the acquisition of Talon.One for 750 million euros in cash and reported Q1 revenue growth of 20% reaching 620.8 million euros.

AEROPORTS DE PARIS (ADP) :

French airport operator Groupe ADP announced the sale of securities representing 3.4% of GAL's equity share capital to GMR Group for around 924 million euros and proposed a special dividend of 0.8 euros per share for 2025, with a potential additional special distribution of 1.0 euro per share upon deal completion.

BOLLORE :

French conglomerate Bollore reported Q1 2026 revenue rose 6.5% to 815 million euros and proposed an exceptional dividend of 1.5 euros per share.

CARMILA :

French shopping center operator Carmila reported Q1 gross rental income of 112.3 million euros and proposed a cash dividend of 1.36 euros per share for 2025, up 9%.

CLARIANE :

French elderly care operator Clariane reported Q1 2026 organic revenue up 4.9% to 1,336 million euros.

FNAC DARTY :

French electronics and entertainment retailer Fnac Darty reported Q1 revenue of 2,310 million euros with gross margin rate up 10 basis points like-for-like and confirmed its 2026 and 2030 outlook.

GROUPE SEB :

French small appliances maker Groupe SEB reported Q1 2026 sales of 1,885 million euros, up 2.7% like-for-like, with operating result from activity up 42% to 72 million euros.

LISI :

French aerospace fasteners maker LISI reported Q1 2026 revenue of 468 million euros, up 10.9% on a like-for-like basis, with a very solid aerospace order backlog.

SAINT-GOBAIN :

French building materials maker Saint-Gobain reported Q1 sales of 11.1 billion euros, down 2.3% in local currencies and like-for-like, and confirmed its 2026 outlook targeting an EBITDA margin of over 15.0%.

TIKEHAU CAPITAL :

French alternative asset management company Tikehau Capital reported Q1 group assets under management of 53 billion euros, up 7% versus last year, with net new money of 1.0 billion euros raised across private markets strategies.

UNIBAIL-RODAMCO-WESTFIELD :

European shopping center operator Unibail-Rodamco-Westfield reported Q1 proportionate total turnover of 908.2 million euros with tenant sales up 5.0% and confirmed its 2026 adjusted recurring earnings per share guidance of 9.15 to 9.30 euros.

VALEO :

French auto parts maker Valeo reported Q1 sales of 5.12 billion euros, above the 5.01 billion euros consensus, and confirmed its 2026 outlook.

VINCI :

French construction and concessions group Vinci reported stable Q1 revenue at 16.3 billion euros, up 1% at constant exchange rates, with net financial debt improved by 1.4 billion euros year-on-year and confirmed its 2026 outlook.

data.title: Celebi Hava Servisi Says It Will Exit Tanzania Operations After Airport Concession Expiry
data.description:
ÇELEBİ HAVA SERVİSİ :

  • SAID ON THURSDAY OPERATIONS OF ITS TANZANIAN UNIT CELEBI TANZANIA AVIATION SERVICES, PROVIDES GROUND HANDLING AND AIR CARGO SERVICES AT JULIUS NYERERE INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT IN DAR ES SALAAM, TANZANIA, WILL NOT CONTINUE UNDER THE CURRENT STRUCTURE

  • FOLLOWING EXPIRY OF CONCESSION AGREEMENT FOR AIRPORT WHERE AFOREMENTIONED SUBSIDIARY OPERATES, OPERATIONS IN RELEVANT MARKET WILL NOT CONTINUE UNDER CURRENT STRUCTURE

  • CELEBI SAYS IT HAD DECIDED NOT TO PURSUE OPERATIONS IN TANZANIAN MARKET AFTER A COMPREHENSIVE EVALUATION OF OPERATING FRAMEWORK, IN LINE WITH ITS LONG-TERM VALUE CREATION STRATEGY

  • THE UNIT, CELEBI TANZANIA AVIATION SERVICES LTD, HAS A LIMITED CONTRIBUTION TO CONSOLIDATED RESULTS, ACCOUNTING FOR ABOUT 0.2% OF TOTAL REVENUE AND 0.5% OF TOTAL ASSETS AS OF DEC. 31, 2025, WHILE ITS LOSS IMPACT ON NET PROFIT STOOD AT AROUND 2.1%

  • THE EXIT WOULD NOT HAVE A MATERIAL IMPACT ON CONSOLIDATED FINANCIALS OR PROFITABILITY

  • CELEBI SAYS IT MAINTAINS A STRONG OPERATIONAL PRESENCE ACROSS EUROPE, ASIA AND AFRICA, AND CONTINUES TO ASSESS GROWTH OPPORTUNITIES ON THE CONTINENT FOLLOWING ITS RECENT ENTRY INTO THE KENYAN MARKET

data.title: Key facts: Infosys beats Q4 profit; FY27 1.5–3.5% guidance; CEO equity
data.description:
• Infosys forecasts FY27 revenue growth of 1.5%–3.5% and operating margin of 20%–22%; cites AI and Europe as main revenue drivers, with AI services >5.5% of revenue.1
  • Infosys Q4: net profit ₹85.01B vs est ₹74.65B; revenue ₹464.02B, +13.4% YoY. Demand from banking, energy and communications drove the revenue gain.23
  • Choice reiterated BUY on Infosys (INFY), cut target to INR 1,500. Cited stable quarterly results, record $15bn deal wins, 24% TCV growth, modest near‑term revenue growth ~3.5% and FY27 ~2.5% CC.4
  • INFY announced partnerships to modernize supply chains, upgrade digital infrastructure and banking IT, and scale autonomous and agentic AI deployments with multiple tech and research partners.5
  • Infosys said sequential deal wins fell because last quarter included a single $1+ billion contract; absence of a comparable $1B+ deal this quarter cut reported deal value.6
  • Infosys Q4: US dollar revenue and EBIT slightly below CNBC-TV18 poll; rupee revenue matched poll. Reported earnings marginally softer than survey for the quarter.7
  • Infosys granted equity awards to CEO Salil Parekh effective May 2, 2026, across performance, ESG-linked, TSR and ownership programs; vesting subject to conditions and units tied to market price.8

data.title: Car parts group Forvia's quarterly revenue drops on lower China sales
data.description:
French car parts supplier Forvia on Friday reported a 2.2% drop in its first-quarter sales, excluding currency effects, impacted by a decline in China.

Quarterly sales fell to 5.14 billion euros ($6.00 billion) from 5.49 billion euros in the same period last year.

The group reported growth in all of its regions except China, where sales were down 23.5% from last year, excluding the effects of currency exchange rates.

Forvia was impacted by an unfavourable customer mix in China, with a significant drop in production at automaker BYD, it said.

It still outperformed the 3.4% decline seen in global automotive production, according to S&P Global Mobility forecasts published this month.

"At the same time, we have continued to make progress on the planned divestiture of our Interiors business, which we expect to materialize in the near term," CEO Martin Fischer said in a press release.

The company also confirmed its guidance for 2026.

($1 = 0.8563 euros)
